NVDA.title: Forget Nvidia: Why 'Worker Bees' Like Micron Technology, Texas Instruments Are Real AI Winners
Jim Cramer Says Nvidia Sellers 'Disappeared' As Stock Pops On Monday: What You Should Know

INTC.title: Jim Cramer Says Nvidia Sellers 'Disappeared' As Stock Pops On Monday: What You Should Know

macro_events.event_summary: BP beat Q1 profit expectations, citing higher fuel prices driven by the Iran war, signaling broad upside for integrated oil majors and refiners benefiting from elevated crude and crack spreads.
primary_drivers: Elevated crude oil prices from Iran war risk premium boosting upstream realized prices
Wider refining crack spreads from supply disruption fears lifting downstream margins
Peer read-through: BP beat sets positive expectations for Western oil major Q1 prints

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated supermajor with similar upstream/downstream mix to BP; BP's beat signals XOM's upcoming Q1 will likely show similar leverage to elevated Brent and crack spreads. Direct peer read-through ahead of XOM earnings.
Closest US integrated peer to BP with significant upstream exposure; BP's confirmation that Iran-driven prices boosted profits supports CVX Q1 expectations. Peer-rotation flow.
Pure-play US refiner; BP's downstream strength implicates wider crack spreads from Iran disruption fears, directly boosting Marathon's refining margins which are ~90% of earnings.
Largest independent US refiner; same crack spread tailwind BP cited in its downstream segment translates more purely to Valero given no upstream offset.
Pure-play US E&P with high leverage to Brent prices; Iran war premium directly lifts realized prices with no downstream dilution.
US E&P with high operating leverage to crude prices and Permian-weighted production; Iran risk premium flows straight through to cash flow.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline operating costs; BP confirming sustained higher fuel prices means airlines face ongoing margin compression with limited near-term hedging relief.
Same jet fuel cost exposure as DAL with less hedging than legacy peers; sustained Iran-driven crude strength directly hits unit costs.
Diesel and jet fuel are major opex line; persistent oil strength from geopolitical premium pressures margins on contracts not yet repriced with fuel surcharges.
Commodity chemicals producer with naphtha/natural gas feedstock costs; sustained energy price elevation squeezes spreads if it can't pass through.

headlines: Oil major BP beats profit expectations as Iran war boosts fuel prices
